full_text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Godly Play is the program that we use in children's formation at St. John's Cathedral.<|5.80|> <|6.70|> In Godly Play, a storyteller will present a story or a parable of Jesus.<|11.58|> <|12.50|> And then after the story is read aloud, pause and ask carefully, quietly, and intentionally a wondering question.<|22.46|> <|22.46|> For example, I wonder what part of the story you like best.<|29.10|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.72|> I know what part of the Easter Day story I like best. I love the part where in John's gospel,<|9.08|> <|9.08|> the risen Christ says Mary's name, and that's when she recognizes the risen Christ.<|16.30|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> risen Christ.<|0.74|> <|1.30|> Just before that, Mary Magdalene has come to the tomb, and in John's gospel, it's dark,<|7.18|> <|7.34|> pitch black.<|8.36|> <|9.32|> And there at the tomb, she encounters something or someone or a sound, a rustling.<|15.90|> <|16.74|> There's a voice, and she supposes that she is speaking with the gardener.<|21.36|> <|21.90|> She supposes that in part because there is a garden nearby, according to John.<|26.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.72|> John's also thinking theologically and deeply about other biblical echoes,<|6.16|> <|6.64|> and it's the echo of the Garden of Eden in Genesis 1, of course.<|11.42|> <|12.24|> A quick dialogue ensues.<|14.56|> <|15.02|> Mary Magdalene doesn't know exactly what's going on<|18.42|> <|18.42|> until finally the risen Christ says her name.<|21.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.98|> Mary. I love this part best because, first of all, I love and often pay attention to how people<|8.42|> <|8.42|> say my own name, whether with tenderness or with kindness or with energy and curiosity.<|15.08|> <|15.48|> We all know how a name is spoken matters a lot, and I really love imagining how the risen Christ<|24.20|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> says it. Did Christ say it in a whisper? Did Christ say it with confidence and<|6.76|> <|6.76|> loudly? I'm certain that Christ did say it with absolute tenderness, speaking from<|14.88|> <|14.88|> the other side of death.<|17.60|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We preach these sermons, we pray these prayers, we have these Holy Week rites.<|8.46|> <|9.22|> We are now recording all of this online, and the point behind all of it<|13.82|> <|13.82|> is not simply for someone to watch clergy or other lay leaders pray and sing.<|20.54|> <|20.54|> The point behind all of this is<|22.70|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> is ultimately to inspire your own prayers, your own song, your own reflection.<|8.36|> <|9.48|> So I wonder, what part of the Easter story do you like best?<|15.72|> <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And when you know what part that is and can think about it and speak about it<|7.46|> <|7.46|> and own your favorite part deep in your bones, deep in your heart, it will be Easter Day.<|15.08|>

segments.text: <|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> Godly Play is the program that we use in children's formation at St. John's Cathedral.<|5.80|>
<|6.70|> In Godly Play, a storyteller will present a story or a parable of Jesus.<|11.58|>
<|12.50|> And then after the story is read aloud, pause and ask carefully, quietly, and intentionally a wondering question.<|22.46|>
<|22.46|> For example, I wonder what part of the story you like best.<|29.10|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.72|> I know what part of the Easter Day story I like best. I love the part where in John's gospel,<|9.08|>
<|9.08|> the risen Christ says Mary's name, and that's when she recognizes the risen Christ.<|16.30|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> risen Christ.<|0.74|>
<|1.30|> Just before that, Mary Magdalene has come to the tomb, and in John's gospel, it's dark,<|7.18|>
<|9.32|> And there at the tomb, she encounters something or someone or a sound, a rustling.<|15.90|>
<|16.74|> There's a voice, and she supposes that she is speaking with the gardener.<|21.36|>
<|21.90|> She supposes that in part because there is a garden nearby, according to John.<|26.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.72|> John's also thinking theologically and deeply about other biblical echoes,<|6.16|>
<|6.64|> and it's the echo of the Garden of Eden in Genesis 1, of course.<|11.42|>
<|12.24|> A quick dialogue ensues.<|14.56|>
<|15.02|> Mary Magdalene doesn't know exactly what's going on<|18.42|>
<|18.42|> until finally the risen Christ says her name.<|21.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.98|> Mary. I love this part best because, first of all, I love and often pay attention to how people<|8.42|>
<|8.42|> say my own name, whether with tenderness or with kindness or with energy and curiosity.<|15.08|>
<|15.48|> We all know how a name is spoken matters a lot, and I really love imagining how the risen Christ<|24.20|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> says it. Did Christ say it in a whisper? Did Christ say it with confidence and<|6.76|>
<|6.76|> loudly? I'm certain that Christ did say it with absolute tenderness, speaking from<|14.88|>
<|14.88|> the other side of death.<|17.60|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> We preach these sermons, we pray these prayers, we have these Holy Week rites.<|8.46|>
<|9.22|> We are now recording all of this online, and the point behind all of it<|13.82|>
<|13.82|> is not simply for someone to watch clergy or other lay leaders pray and sing.<|20.54|>
<|20.54|> The point behind all of this is<|22.70|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> is ultimately to inspire your own prayers, your own song, your own reflection.<|8.36|>
<|9.48|> So I wonder, what part of the Easter story do you like best?<|15.72|>
<|startoftranscript|><|en|><|transcribe|><|0.00|> And when you know what part that is and can think about it and speak about it<|7.46|>
<|7.46|> and own your favorite part deep in your bones, deep in your heart, it will be Easter Day.<|15.08|>
